items.title: Amit Shah Inaugurates Exhibition of Buddha Relics in Leh
items.whyInNews: Union Home and Cooperation Minister Amit Shah inaugurated the exhibition of Buddha relics in Leh, Ladakh, on May 2, 2026. The relics have been brought back to Ladakh after 75 years.
items.summary: Amit Shah inaugurated the exhibition of Buddha relics in Leh, which is a significant event for the people of Ladakh. The relics have been brought back to Ladakh after 75 years, and this event is expected to promote tourism and cultural exchange in the region. The exhibition is a part of the government's efforts to preserve and promote India's rich cultural heritage.
keyPoints: Amit Shah inaugurated the exhibition of Buddha relics in Leh on May 2, 2026
The relics have been brought back to Ladakh after 75 years
The exhibition is expected to promote tourism and cultural exchange in the region
The event is a part of the government's efforts to preserve and promote India's rich cultural heritage
importantPoints: The Ministry of Culture was established in 1999 and is headquartered in New Delhi
The Antiquities and Art Treasures Act, 1972, regulates the export and import of cultural artifacts in India
The exhibition of Buddha relics is a significant event in the history of Ladakh, which has a rich cultural heritage
The event is expected to promote cultural exchange between India and other countries, particularly in the Buddhist world

The government's efforts to preserve and promote India's cultural heritage are in line with the provisions of the Constitution of India, which emphasizes the importance of preserving and promoting the country's cultural diversity
items.examRelevance: GK: Indian Culture and Heritage, Polity: Constitutional Provisions for Cultural Preservation

items.title: India and Tanzania Hold 5th Joint Trade Committee Meeting
items.whyInNews: India and Tanzania held the 5th Joint Trade Committee meeting in Dar es Salaam on May 1, 2026, to discuss ways to strengthen bilateral trade and economic cooperation.
items.summary: The meeting was co-chaired by the Commerce Secretary of India and the Minister of Industry and Trade of Tanzania. The two countries discussed ways to increase trade and investment in areas such as agriculture, manufacturing, and services. The meeting also focused on promoting economic cooperation between the two countries, particularly in the areas of trade facilitation and infrastructure development.
keyPoints: India and Tanzania held the 5th Joint Trade Committee meeting in Dar es Salaam on May 1, 2026

India and Tanzania have a long history of trade and economic cooperation, with India being one of Tanzania's largest trading partners
The meeting is expected to promote economic cooperation between the two countries, particularly in the areas of trade facilitation and infrastructure development

items.title: NCB Seizes 349 kg of Cocaine in Mumbai
items.whyInNews: The Narcotics Control Bureau (NCB) seized 349 kg of cocaine in Mumbai on May 1, 2026, in a major operation against drug trafficking.
items.summary: The NCB seized the cocaine, which is estimated to be worth Rs 1,745 crore, in a raid conducted in the Mumbai area. The operation was part of the NCB's efforts to crack down on drug trafficking and smuggling in the country. The seizure is one of the largest in recent times and is expected to have a significant impact on the drug trade in India.

The cocaine is estimated to be worth Rs 1,745 crore

items.title: Sikkim Becomes First Paperless State Judiciary in India
items.whyInNews: Sikkim has become the first paperless state judiciary in India, with the introduction of a digital case management system.
items.summary: The digital case management system was launched by the Chief Justice of India, who praised the efforts of the Sikkim judiciary in adopting technology to improve the administration of justice. The system is expected to increase transparency and efficiency in the judicial process, and to reduce the time taken to dispose of cases. The move is part of the government's efforts to promote digitalization and e-governance in the country.
keyPoints: Sikkim has become the first paperless state judiciary in India
The digital case management system was launched by the Chief Justice of India
The system is expected to increase transparency and efficiency in the judicial process

importantPoints: The Supreme Court of India was established in 1950 and is headquartered in New Delhi

items.title: BIS Publishes Six New Standards for Priority Assistive Products
items.whyInNews: The Bureau of Indian Standards (BIS) has published six new standards for priority assistive products, with the aim of promoting accessibility and inclusivity in the country.
items.summary: The six new standards cover a range of products, including wheelchairs, hearing aids, and Braille displays. The standards are expected to promote accessibility and inclusivity in the country, particularly for persons with disabilities. The move is part of the government's efforts to promote accessibility and inclusivity, particularly in the areas of education, employment, and healthcare.

items.title: India to tap augmented solar capacity, coal to weather El Nino, summer-related power demand
items.whyInNews: India attained its peak demand for power on April 25, with solar plants accounting for 21.5% of the power generated, while coal plants stood at 66.9%. The government plans to tap augmented solar capacity and coal to meet the summer-related power demand.
items.summary: The Indian government is planning to tap augmented solar capacity and coal to meet the summer-related power demand. On April 25, India attained its peak demand for power, with solar plants accounting for 21.5% of the power generated. The government aims to ensure a stable power supply despite the challenges posed by El Nino and summer-related power demand.
keyPoints: India attained its peak demand for power on April 25
Solar plants accounted for 21.5% of the power generated
Coal plants stood at 66.9% of the power generated
The government plans to tap augmented solar capacity and coal
importantPoints: The Ministry of Power was established in 1992 and is headquartered in New Delhi
The Electricity Act of 2003 regulates the power sector in India
India has set a target of achieving 500 GW of non-fossil fuel-based power generation capacity by 2030
The country ranks 3rd in renewable energy installations, with a total capacity of over 150 GW
The government's plan to tap augmented solar capacity and coal is crucial to ensure a stable power supply and meet the increasing demand
items.examRelevance: GK: Science and Technology, Economy: Energy Sector

items.title: England widen lead over India in women's ODI rankings
items.whyInNews: India, the reigning ODI World Cup champions, have slipped in the rankings after suffering a 0-3 series sweep against Australia. England have widened their lead over India in the women's ODI rankings.
items.summary: The Indian women's cricket team has slipped in the ODI rankings after suffering a 0-3 series sweep against Australia. England have widened their lead over India, with the Indian team currently ranked 2nd. The team had won the ODI World Cup title last year, defeating South Africa in the final.

items.title: Thomas Cup: India blank Chinese Taipei 3-0 riding on superb singles wins from Lakshya, Ayush
items.whyInNews: The Indian badminton team has won the Thomas Cup, defeating Chinese Taipei 3-0. Lakshya and Ayush won their singles matches, helping India secure the victory.
items.summary: The Indian badminton team has won the Thomas Cup, defeating Chinese Taipei 3-0. Lakshya and Ayush won their singles matches, with Lakshya coming from behind to win 18-21, 22-20, 21-17. The victory marks a significant achievement for the Indian team, which has been performing well in recent tournaments.

items.title: Nationwide cell broadcast test alert on May 2; people may receive emergency alert on mobile phones
items.whyInNews: The Indian government is conducting a nationwide cell broadcast test alert on May 2, which may result in people receiving an emergency alert on their mobile phones.
items.summary: The Indian government is conducting a nationwide cell broadcast test alert on May 2, as part of its efforts to improve disaster management and emergency response. The test alert may result in people receiving an emergency alert on their mobile phones, which could be accompanied by a loud sound or vibration.

items.title: Pact on foreign language learning set to provide global platform to Rajasthan’s youth
items.whyInNews: The Rajasthan government has signed a Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) with the English and Foreign Languages University, Hyderabad, to provide a global platform to the state's youth through foreign language learning.
items.summary: The Rajasthan government has signed a Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) with the English and Foreign Languages University, Hyderabad, to provide a global platform to the state's youth through foreign language learning. The MoU aims to promote foreign language communication skills among the youth, enhancing their employability and career prospects.

The MoU aims to provide a global platform to the state's youth through foreign language learning
